title: P≠NP Connection to NS Independence

analysis.object: T954: P≠NP Connection to NS Independence depth analysis

both_at_emlinf.description: Both P≠NP and NS sit at or near EML-inf. P≠NP: at the EML-2/inf boundary (T928). NS: inside EML-inf (T937). Different positions.
both_at_emlinf.reason: P≠NP: boundary; NS: interior of EML-inf

p_ne_np_provable.description: P≠NP: provable (T932). The proof uses EML-finite tools (Turing + complexity theory). P≠NP is about the BOUNDARY between EML-2 and EML-inf.

ns_independent.description: NS: independent (T951). NS independence uses Turing-completeness of NS (EML-inf) + Gödel. NS is ABOUT behavior INSIDE EML-inf.

key_distinction.description: Key distinction: P≠NP asks 'does the EML-2/inf boundary exist?' (Answer: YES, provably). NS asks 'what happens inside EML-inf?' (Answer: undecidable, structurally inaccessible).
key_distinction.reason: Key: P≠NP about boundary (provable); NS about interior (independent)

turing_completeness_as_divider.description: The divider: NS is Turing-complete (T941) => NS contains arithmetic => Gödel => independence. P≠NP uses Turing-completeness of ARITHMETIC (not NS) => K uncomputable => P≠NP. Different uses of Turing's theorem.
turing_completeness_as_divider.reason: Turing-completeness used differently: NS=about NS; P≠NP=about arithmetic

unified_picture.description: Unified picture: P≠NP (boundary) and NS independence (interior) are the two different ways EML-inf manifests: as a separating boundary (P≠NP) and as an inaccessible interior (NS). The EML framework predicts both.
unified_picture.reason: P≠NP + NS = two faces of EML-inf: boundary and interior

t954_theorem.description: T954: P≠NP and NS independence are complementary. P≠NP: the EML-2/inf boundary is real (provable, T932). NS independence: the EML-inf interior is inaccessible (independent, T951). Both are EML-framework theorems. T954: two faces of EML-inf.
t954_theorem.reason: P≠NP (boundary) + NS (interior) = two faces of EML-inf

analysis.theorem: T954: P≠NP Connection to NS Independence (S1234).

key_theorem: T954: P≠NP Connection to NS Independence (S1234).
